blurb: London-based image-generation lab. Subject of the first major UK court ruling on copyright/trademark and generative AI — Getty Images v Stability AI (High Court, Nov 2025), a mixed result it largely prevailed in.

publications.title: Stable-Layers: Fine-Tuning Image Layer Decomposition Models with VLM-Scored Reinforcement Learning
publications.summary: A reinforcement-learning framework that fine-tunes a pretrained image layer-decomposition model using only feedback from a vision-language model (VLM), removing the need for paired supervision.

publications.title: OCTOPUS: Optimized KV Cache for Transformers via Octahedral Parametrization Under Optimal Squared Error Quantization
publications.summary: A KV-cache quantization codec for transformers that, across text, video and audio, matches or beats prior rotation codecs at every reported bit width, with the lead growing at extreme compression.

publications.title: Introducing Stable Audio 3 & SAME (Semantically-Aligned Music Autoencoder)
publications.summary: A family of fast latent-diffusion models (small, medium, large) for variable-length audio generation and editing, alongside SAME, a semantically-aligned music autoencoder.

publications.title: Foley Control: Aligning a Frozen Latent Text-to-Audio Model to Video
publications.summary: A lightweight approach to video-guided Foley that keeps pretrained single-modality models frozen and learns only a small cross-attention bridge between a text-to-audio model and video.

publications.title: SViM3D: Stable Video Material Diffusion for Single Image 3D Generation
publications.summary: A framework that predicts multi-view-consistent physically-based-rendering (PBR) materials from a single image by repurposing video-diffusion models for efficient 3D reconstruction.

controversies.title: Getty Images v Stability AI — first major UK High Court ruling on copyright & generative AI
controversies.summary: On 4 November 2025 the High Court (Mrs Justice Joanna Smith, [2025] EWHC 2863 (Ch), case IL-2023-000007) handed down the first major UK judgment on how copyright and trade-mark law apply to generative-AI model development. The result is mixed, not a clean win: Getty had to drop its primary copyright/training claim mid-trial because there was no evidence the training of Stable Diffusion occurred in the UK; Stability then prevailed on the remaining secondary-infringement claim, with the judge holding that 'an AI model such as Stable Diffusion which does not store or reproduce any copyright works (and has never done so) is not an infringing copy'. However, Getty succeeded on a limited trade-mark claim where Stable Diffusion outputs reproduced Getty watermarks in circumstances capable of causing confusion; the judge declined to rule on passing off. Widely read as a setback for rights-holders relying on the UK's secondary-copyright regime, and landing amid the live UK government consultation on copyright and AI.
controversies.rawExcerpt: An AI model such as Stable Diffusion which does not store or reproduce any copyright works (and has never done so) is not an 'infringing copy'. — Mrs Justice Joanna Smith, [2025] EWHC 2863 (Ch)

notes: Worked example for controversy-scope.md (2026-06-24). Attribution checks passed: (1) named-party — Stability AI Ltd is the defendant, not a passing mention; (2) primary-source — official judiciary.uk judgment + PDF, neutral citation [2025] EWHC 2863 (Ch); (3) same-entity — Stability AI Ltd, the Stable Diffusion maker, matches seed slug stability-ai. Tone test: summary refuses to oversimplify 'who won' — it states the dropped training claim, the secondary-infringement win, AND the limited trademark loss, because the honest read is mixed. Related prior interlocutory rulings on BAILII if the table ever wants case history: [2025] EWHC 109 (Ch), [2025] EWCA Civ 749, [2023] EWHC 3090 (Ch). 2026-06-27: filled the previously-empty publications[] (the only feeds-sync-ingested array) with 5 real research outputs harvested from stability.ai/research; each date is the page's real JSON-LD datePublished (verified per-page via curl, not the listing card), not a sitemap/bulk-migration timestamp. Existing controversies/news preserved.

news.title: WPP strategic investment and partnership integrating Stability's visual-media models
news.summary: WPP announced a strategic investment and partnership integrating Stability's visual-media models into WPP's creative platforms (March 2025) — a major ad-industry adoption of generative-image models, landing against the backdrop of Stability's Getty copyright litigation. First-party news index linked; corroborated via public records.
